back: A formal language used to write instructions for a computer.
A programming language that is easier for humans to read and write using English-like commands.

front: Give an example of a high-level language.
back: Python.

front: Give one advantage of high-level languages.
back: They are easier to read, write and debug.

front: Give one disadvantage of high-level languages.
back: Programs written in high-level languages may execute more slowly than programs written in low-level languages.
A language close to machine code that gives more direct control of hardware.
Machine code and assembly language.

front: Give one advantage of low-level languages.
back: They give direct control of hardware and programs can execute very quickly.

front: Give one disadvantage of low-level languages.
back: They are difficult for humans to read, write and debug.
The binary instructions (1s and 0s) that the CPU understands directly.
The part of a machine code instruction that represents the operation to perform.
The part of a machine code instruction that represents data or a memory location.

front: In machine code 01110 11011, which part is the opcode and which part is the operand?
back:
01110 = opcode (instruction)
11011 = operand (data or memory location)

front: What do opcodes and operands represent in machine code?
back:
Opcode = operation to perform
Operand = data or memory location used
A low-level language that uses mnemonics instead of binary.
A short code that represents a machine instruction, such as ADD or LDA.

front: Can the CPU run assembly language directly?
back: No. Assembly language must first be translated into machine code by an assembler.
Software that converts code from one language into another.

front: Why must all programs be translated?
back: Because the CPU can only understand machine code.
A translator that converts assembly language into machine code.
A translator that converts the entire program into machine code before execution.
An executable file.
A file containing machine code that can be run directly by the CPU.

front: When are errors reported in a compiled program?
back: After the whole program has been compiled.
A translator that converts and executes one line of code at a time.
When an error is found.

front: Give one advantage of an interpreter.
back: Errors are found one line at a time, making debugging easier.

front: Give one disadvantage of an interpreter.
back: Programs execute more slowly because they are translated line by line.

front: What is the key difference between a compiler and an interpreter?
back: A compiler translates the whole program at once, while an interpreter translates and executes code line by line.

front: Why is a compiler used for final programs?
back: Because compiled programs usually execute faster and the source code is not visible.
